title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix for message envelopes, binary bodies, schemas, and evolution. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.
Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads with strict runtime schemas, bounded collections, semantic refinements, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 8001 in a recovery drill with stale callbacks from an old channel generation. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Buffer Decoding Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Buffer Decoding Boundary, keep Buffer Decoding Boundary connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 4049 in a recovery drill with stale callbacks from an old channel generation. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
